parsed_questions.question_text: What is the pKa of a sp hybridized carbon atom? a) 25 b) 35 c) 45 d) 50

A) Determine the letter of the molecule with the lowest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). B) Determine the letter of the molecule with the highest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). a) ethane b) propane c) butane d) cyclohexane
Rank the following substituents in order of priority (1 = highest priority). -CH=CH2; -CN; -CH2NH2; -CH2Br.

If a chiral molecule has an absolute configuration of R, which direction does it rotate the plane of polarized light? a) clockwise (dextrorotary) b) counterclockwise (levorotatory) c) it doesn't rotate the plane of polarized light d) can't be determined from the information given
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: d) can't be determined from the information given

parsed_questions.error_comment: Exact equilibrium-arrow choice (which of the two drawn arrow sets was selected) cannot be reliably distinguished from the image; pKa numbers inside the boxes are small and partially unclear.
The detailed structures in the answer boxes and the selected equilibrium-arrow sets are contained only in small hand‑drawn images that are not legible enough to transcribe unambiguously.

parsed_questions.question_text: Rank the following sets of molecules in order of acidity (1 = most acidic). First set of acids: (1) (CH3)2NH with a proton on nitrogen, i.e., dimethylammonium ion; (2) CH3SH with a proton on sulfur, i.e., methanethiol; (3) (CH3)2PH with a proton on phosphorus, i.e., dimethylphosphonium ion; (4) HCl; (5) tert‑butyl cation with a proton on carbon, i.e., a tert‑butyl carbon acid. Assign to each of these five acids an acidity rank number from 1 (most acidic) to 5 (least acidic) using {box_1}, {box_2}, {box_3}, {box_4}, and {box_5} respectively. Second set of acids: (1) protonated methanethiol, CH3S(OH)H+; (2) methanethiol, CH3SH with a proton on sulfur; (3) protonated dimethylamine, (CH3)2NH2+; (4) protonated methanol, CH3OH2+; (5) methanol, CH3OH with a proton on oxygen. Assign to each of these five acids an acidity rank number from 1 (most acidic) to 5 (least acidic) using {box_6}, {box_7}, {box_8}, {box_9}, and {box_10} respectively.

Met-enkephalin, an endorphin, serves as a natural pain reliever that changes or removes the perception of nerve signals. Determine all of the different functional groups present in the Met-enkephalin structure shown.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: amine; phenol; amide; arene; carboxylic acid; sulfide

parsed_questions.question_text: Name the following molecules: A) A substituted octane whose longest chain contains eight carbons and bears substituents that result in the IUPAC name {box_1}. B) A substituted cycloheptane bearing a bromine substituent and a tert‑butyl substituent that result in the IUPAC name {box_2}.

parsed_questions.error_comment: Exact electron-pushing arrows and precise resonance structures cannot be transcribed unambiguously from the drawn images into linear text or SMILES while guaranteeing correctness under the given constraints. The specific atoms circled as nucleophiles in the final structure also cannot be identified with sufficient certainty.

The detailed atomic arrangement in the Newman projections and the exact wording or labels of the circled structures cannot be transcribed unambiguously from the image, so their structures are described only qualitatively.

Specific orientations (axial vs equatorial) and detailed Newman projections cannot be fully reconstructed textually from the image without ambiguity; only the written answer for part d/e is clearly legible ('A has fewer 1,3‑diaxial interactions in its lowest energy conformation').

Individual R/S configurations for the stereocenters are written only as small annotations on the structures and cannot be reliably transcribed; structures themselves cannot be represented unambiguously as text.

parsed_questions.question_text: Indicate the relationships between the two molecules in each of the three given pairs as enantiomers (E), diastereomers (D), or the same molecule (S).
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: First pair: E; Second pair: E; Third pair: D

parsed_questions.question_text: One of the steps in fat biosynthesis is the hydration of crotonate to yield 3-hydroxybutyrate. The reaction occurs by addition of −OH to the si face at C3, followed by protonation at C2, also from the si face. The starting material crotonate (H3C−CH=CH−CO2−) reacts to form 3‑hydroxybutyrate (H3C−CH(OH)−CH2−CO2−). a) Determine whether the top face of each alkene carbon atom in crotonate is re or si. b) Determine the stereochemistry of the chiral center in the 3‑hydroxybutyrate product. c) Determine whether carbon C3 in 3‑hydroxybutyrate is R or S.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: a) At C2 the top face is re and at C3 the top face is si; b) the chiral center in the product is S; c) C3 is S.
